market_note: Stablecoin transfers hit a record $1.79T in June as USDC's volume share widened to ~70%; six federal agencies face a July 18 deadline to finalize GENIUS Act rules.

stablecoin_items.headline: Open USD (OUSD) launches with 140+ backers
stablecoin_items.summary: A new consortium stablecoin backed by Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, BlackRock, Coinbase and Google went live, with Stripe naming it the default stablecoin for Stripe-powered businesses.

stablecoin_items.headline: GENIUS Act rulemaking deadline looms July 18
stablecoin_items.summary: Six U.S. federal agencies must publish final implementing rules for the GENIUS Act stablecoin law by July 18, shaping issuer reserve and licensing requirements.

stablecoin_items.headline: USDC extends lead over USDT in volume race
stablecoin_items.summary: Circle's USDC captured roughly 70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in H1 2026 versus Tether's ~25%, as June volume hit a record $1.79 trillion.

stablecoin_items.headline: Stablecoin market cap slips $1.9B in a week
stablecoin_items.summary: Total stablecoin market cap fell to $311.3B, with Sky Dollar (USDS) leading the decline, down 2.36% to $8.02B amid capital outflows rather than a peg failure.

hack_items.headline: Summer.fi drained for $6M in flash loan exploit
hack_items.summary: An attacker manipulated Lazy Summer vault share accounting via a flash loan, briefly spiking displayed APY to 2,080,000% before withdrawing funds and swapping to DAI.

hack_items.headline: White hats flag $70B systemic flaw in Aptos
hack_items.summary: Security firm Hexens found a bug in Aptos with a near-90% exploit success rate and attack costs of just hundreds of dollars, patched before any funds were taken.

hack_items.headline: H1 2026 hack losses hit $1.32B across 344 incidents
hack_items.summary: Crypto projects lost about $1.32 billion in the first half of 2026; the Drift Protocol hack ($295M) was the largest single incident of the period.

hack_items.headline: Humanity Protocol loses $30M to key compromise
hack_items.summary: Attackers stole private keys backed up on a malware-infected developer machine, underscoring a shift toward compromised-credential attacks over smart contract bugs.
